hcd_data.typology: Converted House-form (27, & 33 Kensington Avenue) House-form, Row
hcd_data.architectural_style: Converted House-form (27, 29, & 33 Kensington Avenue) House-form (31 Kensington Avenue), Row, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The properties at 27, 29, and 33 Kensington Avenue also have value for the modifications made to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations, including a one- storey commercial addition at 33 Kensington Avenue. With its visibly layered built form, each property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. Each property has evolved over time while retaining original building features including cross-gabled rooflines, double-height bay windows, brick cladding and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings (some of the openings at 31 Kensington Avenue have been modified). 33 Kensington Avenue has also retained with decorative wooden gable brackets and pediment. Each property has contextual value because it supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area.

context.street_character: One-way SB | 30 km/h | Meets minimum standards | Secondary commercial spine

assessment.signage: Uncle Vintage Co; King of Kensington; Old & New

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: buff/brown brick with dark gable trim
photo_observations.condition_observed: fair; original brick well-preserved on some units; commercial modifications at ground level
photo_observations.signage_observed: 'UNCLE VINTAGE CO - QUALITY GARMENT' prominent illuminated sign
photo_observations.description: 2.5-storey Victorian bay-and-gable converted to commercial. Buff/brown brick with dark brown gable trim. UNCLE VINTAGE CO. QUALITY GARMENT illuminated sign. Large display window. Rose/flower mural on adjacent wall. Graffiti on lower wall.
photo_observations.brick_colour_observed: buff/yellow brick (foreground building); red-brown brick (background buildings)

windows_observed.gable: small windows in gable peaks (2+ visible across row)
windows_observed.upper_floor: 2 windows per unit; 1-over-1 double-hung style with white frames

photo_observations.roof_type_observed: gable; multiple front-facing gables in continuous row
photo_observations.decorative_elements_observed: decorative brickwork patterns visible on buff brick building (diamond/lozenge brick inserts visible on upper facade); pointed gable peaks with bargeboard; some buildings show original Victorian ornament; large mural on upper wall of one building
photo_observations.storefront_description: 'Uncle Vintage Co - Quality Garment' illuminated sign on foreground building; large storefront windows; security grilles; mural artwork visible on adjacent building featuring a rose/floral design
photo_observations.party_walls_observed: continuous row; party walls throughout; concrete/stucco party wall visible at right
photo_observations.bond_pattern_observed: running bond with decorative brick details

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form (27, & 33 kensington avenue) house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, gable brackets.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: 33 Kensington Avenue has also retained with decorative wooden gable brackets and pediment. Each property has evolved over time while retaining original building features including cross-gabled rooflines, double-height bay windows, brick cladding and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings (some of the openings at 31 Kensington Avenue have been modified).

facade_description: 33 Kensington Ave is a row-house building. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form (27, & 33 kensington avenue) house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, gable brackets.
heritage_summary: 33 Kensington Avenue has also retained with decorative wooden gable brackets and pediment. Each property has evolved over time while retaining original building features including cross-gabled rooflines, double-height bay windows, brick cladding and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings (some of the openings at 31 Kensington Avenue have been modified).

windows_detail.note: Storefront with 'UNCLE VINTAGE CO. QUALITY GARMENT' signage on dark background with white text; large plate glass windows; illuminated interior; adjacent mural on right side (rose/floral design in oranges and browns)
